title: wrightia indica
intro: Wrightia indica, also known as Indian cork tree or Vadadhari, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of wrightin, a compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Wrightia indica include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a remedy for digestive issues. In traditional medicine, Wrightia indica is prepared as a decoction or powder, and can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including tea, capsules, and ointments.
